Every stair, fire-stair and fire-tower shall be at least three feet in clear width throughout, and at all floor levels shall have landings at least three feet six inches in clear width. Every stair, fire-stair and fire-tower shall be completely separated from every other stair, fire-stair and fire-tower and from every public hall and shaft by fireproof walls, with fireproof doors and assemblies, with the doors self-closing and without transoms, at all openings, except that in dwellings two stories or less in height such walls may be fire-retarded on both sides in lieu of being fireproof. The doors giving access to such stairs shall not be held open by any device whatever. Except in the case of an interior enclosed stair separated from and directly accessible to the public hall by a self-closing fireproof door and except as provided in subdivision five, there shall be provided to light and ventilate every stair at every story a window or windows opening on a street, court, yard or space above a setback. At least one such window shall be at least two feet six inches wide and five feet high unless it opens on a street, in which case its minimum height shall be four feet. The aggregate area of such window or windows at each story shall be at least eighteen square feet. On the top story a ventilating skylight may be substituted for a window. At the entrance story or at the roof level a sash door, such as described in section thirty-five, opening to the outer air may be substituted for such window. texts.full_text: the prognosis of the patients with GBM is verypoor and new molecular targets and treatment strategies are urgently needed to combatit. MicroRNA-148a (miR-148a) hasbeen shown to be dysregulated in certain tumor types. However, the role ofmiR-148a in the pathogenesis of GBMis not fully understood. Here we comprehensively analyzed the roles of miR-148a, downstream DLGAP1, and theirinfluence on molecular regulation and tumor progressionmechanismsspecific tohuman glioblastoma (GBM).Our findingshighlight a complexinteractionwheremiRNA-148a can directly target DLGAP1 mRNA fordegradation or inhibit its translation.This downregulation of DLGAP1 by increased levels of miR-148a leads to alterations in cell proliferation,migration,and invasion processes crucial for GBMaggressiveness. Additionally, we demonstratedthat restoration of DLGAP1expression could partiallyrescue the effects induced by overexpression or inhibition ofmiRNA-148a. Further downstream signaling pathways affectedinclude those involved with PI3K/AKT pathwaymodulation whichis essential in cell survival under stress conditions typical within tumors likeGBM environments. Here's an example of how you can achieve this: ```jsx import React, { useState } from 'react'; function App() { const [text, setText] = useState(''); const [output, setOutput] = useState(''); const handleConvert = () => { const array = text.split(','); // convert text to array setOutput(JSON.stringify(array)); }; const handleCopy = () => { navigator.clipboard.writeText(output).then(() => { console.log('Text copied to clipboard'); }); }; return (

); } export default App; ``` In this example, when the user clicks the "Copy to clipboard" button, the `handleCopy` function is called, which uses the `navigator.clipboard` API to write the `output` text to the clipboard. Note that this API is only supported in modern browsers, so you may want to add a fallback for older browsers. Alternatively, you can use a library like `react-copy-to-clipboard` to handle the clipboard copying for you. Also, you can use `document.execCommand('copy')` method, but this method is deprecated and not supported in all browsers. You can also use `useCallback` hook to memoize the `handleCopy` function, to prevent it from being recreated on every render. You can also add a `try-catch` block to handle any errors that may occur when trying to write to the clipboard. You can also use `useEffect` hook to handle the clipboard copying when the component mounts. To think of a work as autonomous (under any conditions) is to misrepresent this general condition: to deny connections. 3.32 For the artwork to avoid the group is to misrepresent the collective condition of art practice. 3.4 The artwork is caught between misrepresenting itself and being misrepresented by others. 3.41 The only way to be true to the possibility of art is to be against 4. The only way to transform art is to act: to do things outside of the logic and presumptions of existing discourse and practice. 4.1 To act is always to do the impossible: to bring about a situation That was inconceivable beforehand. For an act to be genuine, its outcome cannot be known. 4.2 There are times when it is not possible to act: when the absence of possibility is all too keenly present. 4.21 There are times when analysis seems to be the only possible course: the only way of keeping alive the possibility of action in the future. 4.22 Writing can be the pursuit of truth. 4.23 It is not clear whether writing itself could be a kind of act. 4.3 For an artist, there are times when writing seems to be the only possibility of going on: when being articulate is the only possibility of resistance. 5. Geir's role within Kaizer’s extended beyond guitar; he was also instrumental (pun intended) in shaping the sound and direction that made them stand out. His compositions contributed significantly to their early success, including tracks like "Høye Herrer" ("High Men") which became fan favorites for its catchy melody combined with dark themes. Over time as Kaizers Orchestra evolved musically through several albums such as Ompa til du dør (2003), Evig pint i helvete med Kæmper og konger, and Maskineri in 2010. Zahl's contributions remained crucial to their sound; however his focus shifted more towards the background during live performances after some time. Despite stepping back from being a frontman figure within Kaizers Orchestra as they progressed through various phases of creativity together with Janove Ottesen who took on most vocal duties, Geir continued contributing creatively behind scenes. He also worked in other musical projects and explored different avenues such as composing for film scores or producing music independently. Id. In reviewing a claim of excessive sentencing, the trial judge is to be afforded wide discretion in the imposition of sentences within statutory limits, and the sentence imposed by him should not be set aside as excessive in the absence of a manifest abuse of his discretion. State v. Abercrumbia, 412 So.2d 1027 (La.1982); State v. Morgan, 428 So.2d 1215, at page 1216 (La.App. 3rd Cir. 1983), writ denied 433 So.2d 166 (La. 1983)." In the presentence investigation report ordered by the trial judge, the 50 year old defendant is referred to as a fifth offender due to his extensive criminal record dating back to 1950. In sentencing the defendant, the trial judge stated: "... He hasn't had any crimes since the crime against nature that directly affect persons, not crimes of violence, but he's had a constant flow of serious criminal activity; and the Court has to take that into consideration ...," Given the facts of this case, the penalty received by defendant does not appear to be so disproportionate to the crime *182 committed so as to shock this Court's sense of justice. Thus, it cannot be found that the trial judge abused his wide discretion in imposing sentence.[1] This assignment of error lacks merit. ASSIGNMENT OF ERROR NO. 2 The defendant asserts in this assignment of error that the trial court erred in not considering both the mitigating factors of La.C.Cr.P.
